error.description: An unexpected error occurred. Please try again.

tooltip.median: Median is the middle value in sorted prices, more robust than average and less affected by outliers
tooltip.cv: Coefficient of Variation (CV) = StdDev/Mean, measures relative dispersion, smaller values indicate more consistency
tooltip.rangeRatio: Range Ratio = (Max - Min) / Median, reflects relative measure of price volatility

tooltip.iqrDesc: IQR = Q3 - Q1, represents range of middle 50% data
tooltip.outlierDesc: Outliers: data points beyond 1.5x IQR range

tooltip.accuracyDesc: Alignment of price data with actual market prices
tooltip.consistencyDesc: Consistency of data across different time points

tooltip.p50Desc: 50% of requests have latency below this value
tooltip.p95Desc: 95% of requests have latency below this value
tooltip.p99Desc: 99% of requests have latency below this value

tooltip.correlationDesc: Correlation coefficient ranges from -1 to 1, where 1 indicates perfect positive correlation, -1 indicates perfect negative correlation, and 0 indicates no correlation
tooltip.interpretation: The closer the coefficient is to 1, the more consistent the price trends of two oracles

tooltip.volatilityDesc: Volatility measures the intensity of price changes
tooltip.rollingDesc: Rolling volatility uses moving window calculation, reflecting short-term volatility trends

tooltip.scoreCalculation: Overall score is calculated based on weighted multi-dimensional metrics including accuracy, latency, uptime, and consistency
tooltip.rankChange: Rank change compared to previous period

tooltip.historicalMinDesc: Lowest price in selected time range
tooltip.historicalMaxDesc: Highest price in selected time range
tooltip.healthScoreDesc: Comprehensive health score based on data quality, latency, consistency and other metrics
tooltip.stabilityScoreDesc: Stability score reflecting the stability of price fluctuations

tooltip.freshnessDesc: Data freshness indicates time since last update
tooltip.filterDesc: Use filters to quickly locate data with specific status

tooltip.zoomDesc: Use mouse wheel or drag to select area for zooming
tooltip.panDesc: Hold and drag chart to pan

crossChain.title: Cross-Chain Price Comparison
crossChain.deviationAlert: {count} chain(s) detected with price deviation above threshold
crossChain.medianDesc: The middle value in sorted prices, more robust than average
crossChain.iqrDesc: IQR = Q3 - Q1, reflects dispersion of middle 50% data

tooltip.averagePrice: Average of all chain prices, reflecting overall price level
tooltip.medianPrice: Median is the middle value when prices are sorted, more robust than mean
tooltip.highestPrice: Highest and lowest prices among all chains
tooltip.priceRange: Difference between highest and lowest prices, reflecting price volatility range
tooltip.iqr: IQR = Q3 - Q1, reflects the dispersion of price distribution
tooltip.standardDeviation: Standard deviation measures dispersion relative to the mean
tooltip.skewness: Skewness measures symmetry of price distribution, positive means right-skewed
tooltip.kurtosis: Kurtosis measures peakedness of price distribution, positive means more peaked than normal
tooltip.confidenceInterval95: 95% CI means there's 95% probability the true price falls within this range
tooltip.coefficientOfVariation: Coefficient of Variation = StdDev/Mean, for comparing dispersion across different scales
tooltip.consistencyRating: Consistency rating based on standard deviation percentage
tooltip.dataPoints: Total number of historical price data points

crossChain.anomalyDescription: More than 2 std dev from mean
crossChain.iqrExplanationDesc: The IQR (Interquartile Range) method identifies outliers by calculating the 25th percentile (Q1) and 75th percentile (Q3) of the data, and flagging values outside the [Q1 - 1.5×IQR, Q3 + 1.5×IQR] range. This method is robust to extreme values and suitable for price anomaly detection scenarios.
crossChain.outlierDetectionDesc: Values outside [Q1 - 1.5×IQR, Q3 + 1.5×IQR] range are marked as outliers
crossChain.correlationDesc: Pearson correlation coefficient measures the linear correlation between two chain price trends. r = 1 means perfect positive correlation, r = -1 means perfect negative correlation, r = 0 means no linear correlation.
crossChain.normalRangeDesc: All chain prices are within normal range, no anomalies detected. Cross-chain price consistency is good.

anomalyReasons.priceSignificantlyLower: Price significantly lower than other chains
anomalyReasons.priceSignificantlyHigher: Price significantly higher than other chains
anomalyReasons.severeDeviation: Severely deviated from normal range, requires immediate attention
anomalyReasons.highVolatility: This chain has high price volatility

crossChain.legendDoubleClickHint: Double-click to focus, again to restore
crossChain.acfExplanation: ACF Chart: If autocorrelation coefficients exceed confidence intervals (red dashed line), residuals show autocorrelation. Ljung-Box test p-value < 0.05 indicates significant autocorrelation.
crossChain.distributionExplanation: Distribution: Compare empirical distribution with normal distribution. Large differences may violate normality assumptions.
crossChain.residualsHaveAutocorrelation: Residuals show autocorrelation, model may need improvement.
crossChain.residualsNoAutocorrelation: Residuals show no significant autocorrelation, model fits well.
crossChain.pearsonCorrelationDesc: Pearson correlation coefficient measures linear correlation between two chain price trends. r = 1 means perfect positive correlation, r = -1 means perfect negative correlation, r = 0 means no linear correlation.
crossChain.volatilityConeDesc: Shows volatility distribution range across different time windows to assess current volatility level
crossChain.fixedThresholdDesc: Use fixed percentage as anomaly detection threshold
crossChain.dynamicThresholdDesc: Dynamically adjust threshold based on historical volatility (CV)
crossChain.atrThresholdDesc: Use ATR (Average True Range) indicator to calculate dynamic threshold
crossChain.cointegrationDesc: Cointegration analysis detects long-term equilibrium relationships between two chain prices. ADF statistic less than critical value (-2.86) indicates cointegration exists.
crossChain.needMoreData: More historical data required for testing
crossChain.longSpreadAdvice: Spread is at historical low, recommend long spread (buy undervalued asset, sell overvalued asset), expecting spread to revert to mean.
crossChain.shortSpreadAdvice: Spread is at historical high, recommend short spread (sell overvalued asset, buy undervalued asset), expecting spread to revert to mean.
crossChain.neutralAdvice: Spread is within reasonable range, recommend waiting for better entry opportunity.
crossChain.heatmapHint: Click any cell to pin comparison. Hover for detailed spread information.

info.title: About Pyth Accuracy
info.description: Pyth provides high-frequency price data from first-party sources including major exchanges and trading firms. The accuracy analysis shows the deviation between Pyth prices and actual market prices across different market conditions.

api3.title: API3
api3.subtitle: First-party oracle network enabling decentralized API connectivity
api3.description: API3 delivers first-party oracle services through Airnode, allowing API providers to directly serve blockchain data without intermediaries.

firstPartyOracle.title: First-Party Oracle Advantages Comparison
firstPartyOracle.advantagesDesc: API3, as a first-party oracle, obtains data directly from data sources without intermediaries, providing higher transparency, faster response times, and more reliable data quality.

features.firstPartyOraclesDesc: Direct data delivery from API providers, eliminating middlemen and reducing trust assumptions
features.airnodeDesc: Lightweight, serverless oracle node that allows API providers to easily deploy oracles without maintaining infrastructure
features.decentralizedApiConnectivityDesc: Trustless, decentralized API connectivity enabling smart contracts to securely interact with external data sources
features.quantifiableSecurityDesc: On-chain security guarantees with transparent, auditable security parameters and quantifiable risk assessments

advantages.noMiddlemenDesc: Direct data from API providers eliminates intermediary risks
advantages.sourceTransparencyDesc: Know exactly where your data comes from

coveragePool.mechanismDescription: The Coverage Pool provides insurance for oracle data through staked funds. When data anomalies or errors occur, affected users can receive compensation from the pool. Stakers earn yield by providing funds while assuming certain risk exposure.

bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.heatmapSubtitle: Deviation of each chain's price from the baseline (Cosmos Hub)

bandCrossChainPriceConsistency.mechanism4: Price deviation threshold: <0.1% is normal, 0.1%-0.5% is warning, >0.5% requires attention

stakingDistribution.title: Validator Staking Distribution
stakingDistribution.nakamotoDesc: Requires at least {count} validators to control 33.3% of stake
stakingDistribution.hhiDesc: Lower HHI indicates more dispersed staking (ideal < 1500)
stakingDistribution.concentrationWarningDesc: Current network staking is highly concentrated. The top {count} validators can control over 33.3% of the stake. This may reduce network security. It is recommended to monitor staking distribution changes.
stakingDistribution.concentrationReminderDesc: Network staking distribution has some concentration risk. It is recommended to continuously monitor validator staking changes.
stakingDistribution.nakamotoExplanation: Nakamoto Coefficient: How many validators need to collude to control 33.3% of stake. Higher is safer.
stakingDistribution.hhiExplanation: Herfindahl Index (HHI): Measures staking concentration. Below 1500 indicates dispersion, above 2500 indicates high concentration.
stakingDistribution.topNExplanation: Top N Ratio: The total stake ratio of top N validators. Top 3 should not exceed 33%.

explanation.title: Volatility Calculation Explanation
explanation.stdDevDesc: Standard Deviation (σ): Measures price deviation from the mean, σ = √[Σ(xi-x̄)² / n]
explanation.cvDesc: Coefficient of Variation (CV): Ratio of standard deviation to mean, CV = (σ / x̄) × 100%
explanation.cvInterpretation: Lower CV indicates more stable prices, higher CV indicates greater volatility
explanation.rollingWindowDesc: Rolling volatility uses a 5-point window to reflect volatility trend over time
explanation.multiScaleDesc: Multi-scale analysis helps identify volatility characteristics across different periods
explanation.decompositionDesc: Volatility decomposition shows the contribution of short, mid, and long-term volatility to total volatility

explanation.title: Pearson Correlation Coefficient Explanation
explanation.description: Pearson correlation coefficient measures the linear correlation between two oracle price series. The coefficient ranges from -1 to 1, where 1 indicates perfect positive correlation, -1 indicates perfect negative correlation, and 0 indicates no linear correlation. High correlation coefficients indicate that oracle price movements are highly consistent and data quality is reliable.

link-mainnet-2019.title: Chainlink Mainnet Launch
link-mainnet-2019.description: Chainlink oracle network officially launched on Ethereum mainnet, opening a new era of decentralized oracles.

link-google-2019.title: Google Cloud Partnership
link-google-2019.description: Chainlink established partnership with Google Cloud to bring oracle data into BigQuery.

link-swell-2020.title: Chainlink Price Feeds Launch
link-swell-2020.description: Chainlink Price Feeds officially launched, providing high-quality price data for DeFi protocols.

link-ico-boom-2020.title: DeFi Summer Explosion
link-ico-boom-2020.description: DeFi protocols experienced explosive growth, with Chainlink becoming the preferred oracle for most DeFi projects.

link-price-spike-2020.title: LINK Price Hits All-Time High
link-price-spike-2020.description: LINK token price broke $10, with market cap entering top 10 cryptocurrencies.

link-2-0-2021.title: Chainlink 2.0 Whitepaper Released
link-2-0-2021.description: Chainlink 2.0 whitepaper released, introducing the concept of Decentralized Oracle Networks (DONs).

link-keepers-2021.title: Chainlink Keepers Launch
link-keepers-2021.description: Chainlink Keepers launched on mainnet, providing decentralized automation services.

link-ccip-2022.title: CCIP Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol
link-ccip-2022.description: Chainlink CCIP (Cross-Chain Interoperability Protocol) released, enabling cross-chain messaging.

link-staking-2022.title: Chainlink Staking v0.1
link-staking-2022.description: Chainlink Staking v0.1 launched, providing staking mechanism for node operators and token holders.

link-functions-2023.title: Chainlink Functions Released
link-functions-2023.description: Chainlink Functions launched, allowing smart contracts to connect to any Web2 API.

link-staking-v0-2-2023.title: Chainlink Staking v0.2
link-staking-v0-2-2023.description: Staking v0.2 upgrade introduced native token staking and expanded staking pools.

link-svr-2024.title: Smart Value Recapture (SVR)
link-svr-2024.description: Chainlink launched SVR service to help DeFi protocols capture MEV value.

link-alliance-2024.title: Chainlink Partnership Expansion
link-alliance-2024.description: Strategic partnerships established with major financial institutions and traditional enterprises.

link-price-volatility-2024.title: Market Volatility
link-price-volatility-2024.description: Cryptocurrency market experienced significant volatility, Chainlink oracles maintained high availability and accuracy.

analysis.singleUpdateCostDesc: Gas fee required for a single oracle price update (in USD)
analysis.updateFrequencyDesc: Number of price updates per hour by the oracle
analysis.periodicCostDesc: Periodic operating costs calculated based on update frequency
analysis.efficiencyScoreDesc: Score considering cost and frequency, 100 is optimal
analysis.lowFreqHighEfficiency: Oracles with low frequency and low cost typically have higher efficiency scores
